title: Add partial support for size constraints (#5582 partial)
body:
It adds partial support for size constraints of queues and dynamic arrays.
I added it by replacing `size()` method with a normal `rand` variable. Then this variable is passed to the constraint solver and the array is resized before the unconstrained randomization.

Currently, it doesn't work when:
* elements of the array are constrained, because in such a case the values found by the solver are assigned before the resizing (though it should work if the array before the call to `randomize` is not smaller than the size specified in the constraint),
* the size constraint is in `with` clause of `randomize`, because in such a case, the unconstrained randomization is done before the constrained,
* the array is an element of some other array or struct.
